task.title: Plan Efficiency Foundation sprint
task.summary: Introduce a scoped hot-state efficiency foundation covering config schema v4, sprint cohorts, workflow cursor, explicit build DAGs, and GC/checkpoint policy for roadmap/sprint/task agency.

goal.outcome: CodeWiki can scope hot state and agency by roadmap, sprint, or task with explicit budgets, safe parallelism, workflow cursor state, and garbage-collection checkpoints.
acceptance: Config schema v4 documents and loads budgets, agency scopes, parallelism, GC policy, and quiet rebuild defaults.
Roadmap supports a minimal `roadmap -> sprint -> task` cohort model, and graph exposes roadmap/sprint/task scope views.
Runtime workflow cursor records active loop, expected output, scope, and exit gate, while graph exposes it as a derived view.
Build DAG uses explicit `consumes` and `produces` edges across feedback, documentation, implementation, validation, roadmap, and code.
GC/checkpoint policy classifies artifacts as hot, warm, cold, or purgeable and includes sprint-close hooks for consumed builds, pass validations, closed task shards, and expired runtime state.
Agency can plan runs scoped to roadmap, sprint, or task with token, time, cost, write, session, and risk budgets; session-per-sprint spawning is gated by confirmed harness capability and claims.
non_goals: Do not build the visual UI in this sprint.
Do not implement unsafe unbounded autonomous execution.
Do not require all existing flat-roadmap users to manually migrate before compatibility behavior exists.
verification: codewiki_state refresh shows scoped hot-state views and no false reconciliation drift.

summary: Implemented Efficiency Foundation: config schema v4, canonical sprint cohorts, graph/state scope views, workflow cursor, scoped agency budgets/session plan, hot/warm/cold/purge GC view, and quiet rebuild defaults.

evidence: Added feature smoke coverage for sprint normalization, runtime diff table state, and graph panel data; expanded smoke-test coverage for diff table tool registration.
tester.boundary: derive tests or test-design evidence before code changes where practical

evidence: Implemented v4 config fields, sprint normalization/views, workflow cursor, scoped agency budget planning, GC graph view, and quiet rebuild logging.
builder.boundary: change code until tests, roadmap acceptance, and required checks pass

test_design_evidence: Added feature smoke coverage for sprint normalization, runtime diff table state, and graph panel data; expanded smoke-test coverage for diff table tool registration.
code_change_evidence: Implemented v4 config fields, sprint normalization/views, workflow cursor, scoped agency budget planning, GC graph view, and quiet rebuild logging.

acceptance_mapping.criterion: Config schema v4 covers budgets, agency scopes, parallelism, GC, and quiet rebuild defaults.
acceptance_mapping.evidence: .codewiki/config.json, templates.ts, DocsConfig types, and agency planner now expose schema_version 4, budgets, scope defaults, parallelism/session_per_sprint, GC windows, and quiet rebuild defaults.

acceptance_mapping.criterion: Sprint model supports roadmap -> sprint -> task with graph scope views.
acceptance_mapping.evidence: RoadmapFile/readRoadmapFile preserve top-level sprints; roadmap state and graph expose sprint ids, active sprint ids, sprint_task/scope edges, and scope_views for roadmap/sprint/task.

acceptance_mapping.criterion: Workflow cursor exposes active loop, expected output, scope, and exit gate.
acceptance_mapping.evidence: Task session records accept cursor metadata; graph/status/codewiki_state expose generated or runtime workflow_cursor.

acceptance_mapping.criterion: Build DAG edges are integrated into scoped graph state.
acceptance_mapping.evidence: Graph consumes Build Contract v2 consumes/produces fields and exposes build_* edges in graph views/UI.

acceptance_mapping.criterion: GC/checkpoint policy exposes hot/warm/cold/purge planning and sprint-close hooks.
acceptance_mapping.evidence: Graph gc view classifies active tasks/sprints/claims/handoffs/validation as hot and exposes warm/cold/purgeable classes plus sprint_close_hooks.

acceptance_mapping.criterion: Agency planning supports roadmap/sprint/task scopes and bounded autonomy budgets.
acceptance_mapping.evidence: codewiki_agency accepts scope, token/time/cost/write/session/risk budgets, filters open tasks by scope, and emits plan-only session_spawn_plan gated by config, claims, and scope.

closure_brief.user_intent: Make CodeWiki more efficient through scoped hot state: roadmap/sprint/task execution, configurable budgets, workflow cursor, build DAG handoffs, garbage collection, quiet rebuilds, and safe session-per-sprint parallelism.
implemented_changes: Added config schema v4 defaults for budgets, scopes, parallelism, GC, and quiet rebuilds.
Added canonical roadmap sprints and generated graph/roadmap scope views.
Added workflow cursor support in session, graph, status, and codewiki_state.
Added scoped agency planning with token/time/cost/write/session/risk budgets and plan-only session_spawn_plan.
Added hot/warm/cold/purgeable GC graph view and sprint-close hooks.
Made rebuild quiet by default for tool calls while CLI can opt into verbose logs.
acceptance_evidence: codewiki_state and generated graph expose sprint/scope/cursor/GC views after rebuild.
non_goals_preserved: No unsafe autonomous execution or real session spawning was added.
No full project-management suite was added.
Flat roadmap tasks remain readable while sprint grouping is optional.
remaining_risks: Reload Pi to expose new tool/schema changes.

risks: Existing Pi process must reload to expose the new codewiki_diff_table tool and task-specific validation file naming.

handoff.fallback: Use codewiki_state refresh=true and this implementation_build; do not rely on chat transcript memory.

commit.title: feat(efficiency): add scoped sprint agency foundation
commit.body:
Implemented Efficiency Foundation: config schema v4, canonical sprint cohorts, graph/state scope views, workflow cursor, scoped agency budgets/session plan, hot/warm/cold/purge GC view, and quiet rebuild defaults.

Checks: npx tsc --noEmit, node scripts/check-architecture.mjs, npm test
Validation: .codewiki/validation/2026-05-11-implementation-pass-TASK-056.json
Remote publication requires explicit approval; this build is recommendation-only.
